title: Parodia werdermanniana
taxonomy: Parodia werdermanniana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cactus family (Cactaceae). It belongs to the genus Parodia and is a type of small to medium-sized cactus. The classification of Parodia werdermanniana also places it within the subfamily Cactoideae. This species is native to Brazil and is commonly cultivated for its vibrant flowers and attractive, globular growth habit. As a member of the Parodia genus, it is closely related to other species within the group, such as Parodia lenninghausii.

intro: Parodia werdermanniana, also known as Werdermann's Ball Cactus, is a species of flowering cactus that belongs to the Parodia genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Parodia werdermanniana include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
